3.happening: Make a table dp with one slot per stair, 0 through {n}.
3.why: dp[i] will hold the number of ways to reach stair i.

4.happening: Check there is at least one stair.
4.why: We only seed dp[1] when stair 1 exists.
4.invariant: n is at least 1 by the constraints.

5.happening: There is exactly 1 way to reach stair 1.

6.happening: There are at least two stairs — seed dp[2].
6.why: Stair 2 has its own base case.
6.invariant: dp[2] is set only when stair 2 exists.

6.happening: There is only one stair — skip dp[2].
6.invariant: For n = 1 the answer is already dp[1].

7.happening: There are 2 ways to reach stair 2: (1+1) or (2).

8.happening: Walk i through stairs 3 up to {n}.
8.why: Each higher stair is built from the two below it.
8.invariant: Stairs below i are already solved.

9.why: Every climb to stair i ends with a 1-step (from i-1) or a 2-step (from i-2).
9.invariant: dp[i] now holds the ways to reach stair i.

10.happening: Read the ways to reach the top stair {n}: {dp[n]}.
10.why: The top stair's count is the answer.

11.happening: Return {answer} distinct ways to climb {n} stairs.
11.why: The table is fully filled up to the top.

init.happening: Building the table and seeding the base cases.
init.why: Stairs 1 and 2 have known counts to build from.
init.invariant: dp[1] and dp[2] are the starting points.

loop.happening: Sweeping upward, one stair at a time.
loop.why: Each stair depends only on the two below it.
loop.invariant: Every stair below i is already computed.

update.happening: Filling a stair from its two predecessors.
update.invariant: The current cell fills from its two neighbors.

return.happening: Returning the ways to reach the top stair.
